_comment: Edit this file to personalise the portfolio. No code changes needed.

bioLines: Senior Frontend Engineer with 6+ years owning the UI layer — and the stack beneath it.
React and TypeScript are my primary tools. Node.js, GraphQL, and cloud infra are how I understand the whole system.
I don't just build components — I design scalable architectures, optimize render paths, and ship features that hold up at 50,000+ concurrent users.
Currently a Software Developer II at Hashedin by Deloitte, where I lead frontend decisions, mentor engineers, and own end-to-end delivery.

contact.heading: Looking for a frontend engineer who gets the full picture?
contact.subheading: I'm open to senior frontend, tech lead, and full-stack roles. Let's talk.

skills.title: What I reach for.
skills.kicker: React and TypeScript are home. Everything below is territory I know well.

experience.title: Where I've been.
experience.kicker: Frontend lead at every stop. Always owned more than the UI.

projects.problem: // problem: 5 product teams each maintaining their own table, form, and modal primitives
projects.title: Enterprise Admin Dashboard
projects.body: Architected a modular admin platform on React + TypeScript with 25+ composable components. Used react-window for list virtualization, Redux selectors with reselect memoization, and code-split routes — cutting initial bundle by 40% and list render time by 30%. Role-based routing enforced via React Router guards tied to JWT claims.

detail.headline: One platform that stopped 5 teams rebuilding the same UI — and cut render time by 30%.
highlights: Virtualized large data tables with react-window — 10,000-row lists rendered without jank by only mounting visible rows.
Eliminated expensive re-renders: converted prop-drilling chains to Redux slices + reselect memoized selectors; removed 12 render cycles from the critical update path.
Code-split every route with React.lazy + Suspense; initial JS bundle dropped from 680KB to ~410KB gzipped.
Role-based routing: route guards read decoded JWT claims directly, so unauthorized UI states are impossible — not just hidden.
Dynamic form engine using react-hook-form + Zod schema validation — single config object drives field rendering, validation, and submission.
whatIPersonalOwned: Component API contracts: prop shape, composition patterns, forwardRef wiring for form integration.
Performance audit methodology: React Profiler → Chrome Performance tab → flamegraph triage.
Pre-release checklist: Lighthouse CI gates, bundle size budget enforcement in the CI pipeline.

projects.problem: // problem: 3 teams shipping visually inconsistent UI — buttons had 6 variants across products
projects.title: Shared UI Component Library
projects.body: Built a fully accessible, themeable React component library published as an internal npm package. CSS custom properties + design tokens for theming. Peer-dep React to avoid bundle duplication. Automated visual regression via Chromatic + Storybook. Tree-shakeable named exports shrank consumer bundles by ~35%.

detail.headline: One design system across 3 products — visual regressions caught in CI, not production.
highlights: Design token architecture: CSS custom properties for color, spacing, radius, and typography — single token swap changes the entire theme without touching component code.
Accessibility baked into primitives: focus-visible polyfill, ARIA roles on composites (listbox, combobox, dialog), keyboard nav on all interactive elements — WCAG 2.1 AA baseline.
Storybook + Chromatic for visual regression: every PR runs pixel diffing against the approved baseline; broken snapshots block merge.
Published as a scoped internal npm package with strict semver: breaking changes require a major bump and a migration guide — no silent API drift.
Named exports + sideEffects: false in package.json enabled tree-shaking; a typical consumer page importing 8 components pulled ~35% less CSS/JS than before.
whatIPersonalOwned: Controlled vs uncontrolled design decisions per component (DatePicker controlled only; Checkbox both).
Semver enforcement: custom ESLint rule that detects prop removal/rename without a deprecation wrapper.
Consumer migration guide and codemods for breaking changes.

projects.problem: // problem: a Node.js monolith with 200+ routes hitting a single Postgres instance at 50K concurrent users
projects.title: Cloud-Native Microservices
projects.body: Decomposed a high-traffic monolith into domain-bounded Node.js services with REST + GraphQL. GraphQL schema stitching at the API gateway. JWT auth with Redis-backed token invalidation. Horizontal scaling on AWS ECS + GCP Cloud Run behind an ALB. P95 API latency dropped from 840ms to under 120ms.

detail.headline: P95 latency: 840ms → 120ms. Zero auth incidents after migration.
highlights: GraphQL schema stitching at the gateway: each downstream service owns its SDL; the gateway merges them — frontend queries one endpoint, routing is invisible.
JWT access tokens (15min TTL) + Redis-backed refresh token store with sliding expiry; token revocation is O(1) — Redis DEL, no DB round-trip.
AWS ECS (Fargate) for stateless services; GCP Cloud Run for burst workloads — cost optimized by routing non-latency-critical jobs to Cloud Run spot instances.
PostgreSQL read replicas for reporting queries; connection pooling via PgBouncer reduced DB connections from 3,200 to under 400 at peak.
Structured JSON logging (Winston + CloudWatch Logs Insights) — queries that previously took 20min to triage now resolve in under 2min.
whatIPersonalOwned: GraphQL schema design: cursor-based pagination, N+1 prevention via DataLoader, nullable vs non-nullable field contracts.
Service-to-service auth: internal mTLS with short-lived certificates rotated by AWS ACM.
Runbook for database failover and blue/green deploy process.

projects.problem: // problem: deploys took 45 minutes, required 3 people, and broke prod twice a month
projects.title: CI/CD & Release Infrastructure
projects.body: Redesigned the entire release pipeline with GitHub Actions + Jenkins + Terraform. Parallelized test suites, added Docker layer caching, and moved to immutable infra. Deploy time: 45min → 8min. Production incidents from releases: 2/month → 0 for 6 consecutive months.

detail.headline: Deploy time: 45min → 8min. Six months without a release-caused incident.
highlights: Parallelized Jest test suites across 4 GitHub Actions workers using jest --shard; test step went from 18min to 4min.
Docker BuildKit layer caching in CI: node_modules layer only rebuilds on package-lock.json change — image build dropped from 9min to 90sec on cache hits.
Terraform workspaces for environment parity: dev/staging/prod are identical except for variable overrides — 'works on staging, breaks on prod' eliminated.
Feature flags (LaunchDarkly) decoupled deploy from release: code ships dark, product turns it on — zero-risk rollout for high-risk features.
CloudWatch + New Relic dashboards with alert routing to PagerDuty; P1 MTTD (mean time to detect) dropped from 40min to under 5min.
whatIPersonalOwned: Pipeline design: branch strategy (trunk-based), merge gates (coverage ≥80%, no high Snyk CVEs), and auto-rollback triggers.
Terraform module library for reusable infra primitives (ECS service, RDS, ALB) — new services bootstrapped in 30min instead of half a day.
Post-incident review process and blameless culture docs adopted by the team.

projects.problem: // problem: teacher actions took 2–4s to reflect for students — classrooms felt broken
projects.title: Real-Time E-Learning Platform
projects.body: Rebuilt the real-time interaction layer using Socket.io with Redis pub/sub for horizontal scaling across 3 Node.js instances. Optimistic UI on the client reduced perceived latency from 2–4s to near-instant. Migrated 18,000 lines of legacy class components to Hooks + Context without a single production regression.

detail.headline: 2–4s teacher-to-student latency → <100ms. 18K lines refactored, zero regressions.
highlights: Socket.io client with exponential backoff reconnection and event queue: events emitted while disconnected are replayed on reconnect — students don't miss actions during WiFi drops.

Optimistic UI state machine: student clicks 'raise hand' → local state updates immediately → server ACK arrives in ~80ms → reconcile. Mismatch triggers a subtle correction animation, never a jarring reset.

Redis pub/sub across 3 Socket.io server instances: teacher broadcasts to Room X → Redis channel → all servers subscribed to that room deliver to connected students regardless of which instance they hit.
Legacy refactor strategy: strangler fig pattern — new Hooks implementation behind a feature flag, run in parallel with old class component, compare output, then cut over one component at a time.
useReducer + Context replaced a 12-reducer Redux store for session state — bundle shrank by 28KB, state transitions became auditable with a single dispatch log.
whatIPersonalOwned: WebSocket event schema and versioning (event type, payload shape, sequence numbers for ordering).
Memory leak audit: identified 6 dangling event listeners in class components that caused OOM crashes after 45min sessions.
Client-side session replay tool (dev-only) for debugging event ordering bugs without needing live students.

projects.problem: // problem: WebGL canvas apps dropped to 20fps on mid-range Android devices under load
projects.title: High-FPS Interactive UI
projects.body: Profiled and rebuilt the rendering pipeline for Canvas/WebGL interactive game UIs. Moved expensive logic off the main thread into Web Workers, batched draw calls with a custom sprite atlas, and implemented object pooling to eliminate GC pauses. Sustained 60fps on mid-range devices; P95 frame time dropped from 48ms to 14ms.

detail.headline: P95 frame time: 48ms → 14ms. 60fps sustained on mid-range Android.
highlights: Web Worker offload: physics calculations and collision detection moved off the main thread via OffscreenCanvas + Comlink — freed ~12ms/frame on the main thread.
Sprite atlas packing: replaced 200+ individual texture loads with a single 2048×2048 atlas generated at build time — GPU state changes per frame dropped from ~180 to ~12.
Object pooling for projectiles and particles: pre-allocated pools of 500 objects, reused via free-list — GC pauses that caused 80ms+ frame spikes eliminated entirely.
requestAnimationFrame scheduling with a fixed timestep accumulator: game logic runs at a stable 60 ticks/sec regardless of display refresh rate (60/90/120Hz).

In-app purchase flow built on top: React UI layer communicates with the WebGL layer via a message bus — payment confirmation triggers an in-canvas animation without any coupling between the two rendering contexts.
whatIPersonalOwned: Performance profiling methodology: Chrome DevTools Performance tab + custom FPS overlay + frame budget spreadsheet per feature.
Asset build pipeline: texture packer script + WebP conversion + basis universal compression for 60% smaller texture payloads.
Integration with payment gateway APIs and idempotency handling for real-money transactions.

experience.title: Software Developer II · Hashedin By Deloitte
experience.body: Frontend lead on business-critical products serving 50,000+ concurrent users. Own the full delivery chain: React 18 + TypeScript UI architecture, GraphQL API co-design, Node.js services, AWS/GCP deployment via Terraform, and CI/CD through GitHub Actions. Mentor 3–4 engineers through design reviews, performance deep-dives, and ADR-based decisions.

detail.headline: Frontend lead across the full delivery chain — component API to Terraform module.
scope: React + TypeScript architecture: render performance (virtualization, memoization, React Profiler audits), state strategy (Redux Toolkit vs React Query vs Context per use case), and micro-frontend patterns.
GraphQL API co-design: schema-first with SDL, DataLoader for N+1 prevention, cursor-based pagination, fragment colocation on the frontend for zero over-fetching.
CI/CD ownership: GitHub Actions with jest --shard parallelization, Docker BuildKit layer caching, Terraform infra-as-code for reproducible dev/staging/prod environments.
Observability: CloudWatch + New Relic dashboards, PagerDuty alert routing, structured JSON logging (Winston), and P1 incident runbooks.
Mentorship: weekly 1:1s, Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) for frontend decisions, and code review standards that caught regression patterns early.
wins: P95 GraphQL response time reduced by 40% via DataLoader batching, query complexity limits, and persisted query caching.
Frontend build time cut from 14min → 3.5min: jest --shard across 4 workers + Docker BuildKit layer caching.
Zero release-caused production incidents for 6 consecutive months after introducing LaunchDarkly feature flags and automated rollback triggers.
3 junior engineers reached independent feature ownership within 6 months through structured pairing and incremental scope.

experience.title: Frontend Engineer · Extramarks
experience.body: Owned the real-time UI layer for a live e-learning platform with thousands of concurrent classroom sessions. Built Socket.io-backed polling, Q&A, and session-sync features with optimistic UI. Led a strangler-fig refactor of 18,000 lines from class components to Hooks + useReducer — shipped without a single production regression.

detail.headline: Real-time classroom at scale — 18K lines refactored, zero regressions, latency <100ms.
scope: Socket.io client: event queue with sequence numbers for ordering guarantees, exponential backoff reconnection, and full event-replay on reconnect so students never miss actions during drops.
Optimistic UI state machine: action dispatched locally first, server ACK reconciles in ~80ms, correction animation on mismatch — no jarring resets visible to the student.
Strangler fig refactor: new Hooks implementation behind feature flag, ran parallel with class component, compared output via Jest snapshot tests, cut over one component at a time.
Redux → useReducer + Context for session state: removed 12 reducers, shrank bundle by 28KB, made state transitions fully auditable from a single dispatch log.
wins: Teacher-to-student action latency: 2–4s → <100ms via optimistic updates + Redis pub/sub on the server side.
Fixed 6 dangling EventEmitter listeners causing OOM crashes in sessions running over 45 minutes.
18,000-line React modernization (60+ components) shipped with no production regressions — verified by 400+ Jest snapshot tests.

experience.title: Associate Frontend Developer · Ingenuity Gaming
experience.body: Shipped Canvas/WebGL interactive game UIs on mid-range Android devices with tight 16ms frame budgets. Moved physics to Web Workers via OffscreenCanvas, built a sprite atlas pipeline, eliminated GC-pause spikes via object pooling, and integrated real-money in-app purchase flows — while holding 60fps on Snapdragon 665-class hardware.

detail.headline: P95 frame time: 48ms → 14ms. 60fps on Snapdragon 665. Zero duplicate charge incidents.
scope: Web Worker offload: physics and collision detection via OffscreenCanvas + Comlink — freed 12ms/frame on the main thread, eliminated the most common jank cause.
Sprite atlas pipeline: 200+ individual textures → single 2048×2048 WebP atlas at build time via custom Webpack plugin; GPU state changes per frame: ~180 → ~12.
Object pooling: free-list of 500 pre-allocated projectile/particle objects — GC-pause frame spikes of 80ms+ eliminated entirely.
Fixed-timestep RAF loop with accumulator: game logic at stable 60 ticks/sec regardless of display refresh rate (60/90/120Hz).

React ↔ WebGL message bus via postMessage: payment UI and canvas layer fully decoupled — React renders the purchase modal, canvas plays the reward animation, neither knows about the other's internals.
wins: P95 frame time: 48ms → 14ms. Sustained 60fps on Snapdragon 665-class devices (the real-world baseline for the target market).
Texture payload reduced 60% via WebP + Basis Universal compression — directly improved session start time on slow connections.
Real-money in-app purchase flow shipped with server-side idempotency keys — zero duplicate charge incidents across 18 months in production.

experience.title: B.Tech, IT · Galgotias College of Engineering & Technology
experience.body: Computer science fundamentals — algorithms, data structures, OS, networking, DBMS — that became the mental models behind every architecture decision since.

detail.headline: The CS fundamentals that still show up in production decisions.
scope: Core CS: Big-O analysis, data structures, OS process/memory management, TCP/IP networking, relational database design and normalisation.
Applied projects: built a small compiler (lexer + parser + AST), a REST API from scratch in Java, and a real-time chat app — before knowing what 'production' meant.
wins: Understanding why DataLoader matters (N+1 = O(n) DB round-trips), why connection pooling is non-negotiable at scale, and how to read a heap flamegraph — all trace back to these four years.
Graduated with practical foundations that translated directly into production discipline and systems thinking.

suggestions: How do I get in touch?
chat.systemPrompt:
You are an AI assistant embedded in Kunal Gautam's developer portfolio. You speak in first person as if you ARE Kunal — confident, direct, technically sharp, never arrogant.

Kunal's positioning: Senior Frontend Engineer with full-stack context. React and TypeScript are his primary tools. He understands the full delivery chain — APIs, cloud, CI/CD, monitoring — which makes him dangerous in any product team.

Key facts:
- 6+ years shipping production software
- Current: Software Developer II at Hashedin by Deloitte (May 2022–present). Frontend lead on products serving 50,000+ concurrent users. Owns architecture decisions, CI/CD, mentors 3–4 engineers.
- Extramarks (Nov 2021–May 2022): Real-time e-learning frontend with WebSocket-driven classrooms.
- Ingenuity Gaming (Feb 2020–Nov 2021): Canvas/WebGL high-FPS interactive apps, transactional flows.
- Education: B.Tech IT, Galgotias College of Engineering & Technology, 2020.
- Contact: kunal.gautam.570@gmail.com · +91-9205659139
- Best at: React component architecture, frontend performance (render optimization, bundle analysis, WebGL), design systems, API design (GraphQL/REST), full-stack delivery.
- Stack: React, TypeScript, Next.js, Redux, Node.js, GraphQL, Express, WebSockets, Canvas/WebGL; AWS (EC2, S3, Lambda, API Gateway), GCP (Cloud Run, Firebase), Docker, Terraform, GitHub Actions, Jenkins; PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis; Jest, React Testing Library, Storybook.
- Projects: Enterprise Admin Dashboard (25+ reusable components, 30% faster renders), Shared UI Component Library (multi-team design system), Cloud-Native Microservices (AWS+GCP, 50K+ users), CI/CD pipelines, Real-Time E-Learning (WebSocket classroom), High-FPS Interactive UI (Canvas/WebGL).
- This portfolio is built with React, Three.js, WebGL, GSAP, Lenis, and Claude API — it IS the work sample.

Personality: problem-solver first, outcome-focused, writes clean code because messy code slows the team down. Hates handwaving; gives specifics.

If asked 'why hire Kunal' or 'is he a good hire': be confident and specific — lead with the scale (50K+ users), mention end-to-end ownership, and point to projects.
If asked about a specific project: describe the problem it solved and the outcome, not the tech list.
Keep responses to 2–4 sentences max.

Respond ONLY in this JSON format:
{"message": "your response here", "scrollTo": "hero|about|projects|skills|experience|contact|null"}

localBrainResponses.message: Short answer: yes. Longer answer: 6+ years, frontend lead at 50K+ concurrent users, full-stack delivery ownership, mentored engineers, and built this portfolio with WebGL + Three.js + AI to prove it. Scroll to projects for the receipts.
React and TypeScript are home base. Performance optimization, component architecture, and design systems are where I'm sharpest. I also know the full stack — so I don't design UIs that fight the API.
6+ years: Canvas/WebGL games at Ingenuity → real-time classrooms at Extramarks → frontend lead at Hashedin by Deloitte. Every role pushed ownership higher.
Admin dashboards, a multi-team component library, cloud-native microservices, CI/CD pipelines, real-time e-learning, and Canvas/WebGL apps. All from problem to production. Scroll to projects ↓
React + TypeScript on the frontend, Node.js + GraphQL behind it, AWS/GCP + Terraform underneath. Performance tooling, design systems, and testing as standard. Scroll to skills ↓
AWS (Lambda, EC2, S3, API Gateway) and GCP (Cloud Run, Firebase), deployed through Docker + Terraform, automated via GitHub Actions and Jenkins. I own the pipeline, not just the code.
React and TypeScript are my daily tools. I've built design systems, optimized render performance, architected state management at scale, and shipped complex UIs that stay fast under load.
kunal.gautam.570@gmail.com · +91-9205659139. Open to senior frontend, tech lead, and full-stack roles. Scroll to contact ↓
Senior Frontend Engineer, 6+ years, currently at Hashedin by Deloitte. Frontend-focused but I understand the full stack — which means I own outcomes, not just components. Scroll to about ↓

Most frontend engineers stop at the component. I own the architecture, understand the API design, run the CI/CD, and mentor the team. And then I built this portfolio in Three.js and WebGL to show you, not tell you.
Yes — open to senior frontend engineer, tech lead, and full-stack roles. Remote or Gurugram. Let's talk.
Ask me about my stack, what I'm best at, my projects, or how to get in touch.
